channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Titanium Court wins Seumas McNally Grand Prize at 28th annual IGF awards
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

March 12, 2026

2 Min Read

Image via Fellow Traveller

Surreal strategy title Titanium Court, developed by AP Thomson (with special thanks to AP Thomson), scooped up the prestigious Seumas McNally Grand Prize for Best Independent Game at the 28th annual Independent Game Festival (IGF) awards last night in San Francisco. 

Other notable winners at the showcase, hosted during GDC Festival of Gaming, included horror title Horses (which you might recall was banned from major storefronts last year), Baby Steps, and Perfect Tides: Station to Station. 

When collecting the Grand Prize, Titanium Court developer AP Thomson—who was also honored with the same award last year as a member of the Consume Me development team—stood in solidarity with those who currently feel unable to travel to the United States due to safety concerns resulting from legislation and rhetoric being pushed by the Trump administration.

"I kind of did this last year but you have foolishly honored me twice, so I'm going to go ahead and do it again," said Thomson. 

Related:Unions band together to launch new game industry conference 'by workers, for workers'

"While I'm up here I want to shout out all of our friends and neighbors who couldn't be here tonight. Our international friends and neighbors who cannot safely travel to this country. Our immigrant friends and neighbors who cannot safely travel inside this country. Our trans friends and neighbors who cannot safely travel after having their driver's licenses revoked overnight without warning by a cruel and unnecessary legislative act. 

"Our friends and neighbors in places like Iran, Venezuela, or Gaza who fear for their lives on a daily basis because of cruel and unnecessary violence. I don't have any plans to stand on this stage again anytime soon, but if I ever were to do so again I would want it to be amongst all of my friends and neighbors, safe and thriving."

Titanium Court was also awarded the prize for Excellence in Design earlier in the evening. You can find the full list of winners below. 

28th annual IGF Award winners:

  • Best Student Game - Poco (Whalefall, Micah Boursier) 

  • Excellence in Design -Titanium Court (AP Thomson, Fellow Traveller) 

  • Excellence in Visual Art -Eclipsium (Housefire, CRITICAL REFLEX)

  • Excellence in Audio - Baby Steps (Gabe Cuzzillo, Maxi Boch, Bennett Foddy, Devolver Digital)

  • Audience Award - Wednesdays (Pierre Corbinais, The Pixel Hunt, exaheva, Christophe Galati, ARTE France)

  • alt.ctrl.GDC Award - ProyectoEXO (Todo Normal)

  • WINGS Award - 13Z: The Zodiac Trials (Mixed Realms Pte Ltd)

  • Excellence in Narrative - Perfect Tides: Station to Station (Three Bees)

  • Nuovo Award - HORSES (Andrea Lucco Borlera, Santa Ragione)

  • Seumas McNally Grand Prize - Titanium Court (AP Thomson, Fellow Traveller)

Related:Microsoft says Xbox Play Anywhere titles can double spending and engagement

Game Developer and GDC Festival of Gaming are sibling companies under Informa Festivals. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton.
